judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 covers all four expectations, explicitly flagging the snapshot's staleness (generated_at 2026-07-09T19:25Z) and committing to re-check live usage before relying on it, while Report 2 mentions the snapshot timestamp only in passing and never proposes re-verifying it, making Report 1 the stronger answer overall.

report_1.evidence: Full run = transcript_count × avg_tokens_per_scoring_call = 5,200 × 1,800 = 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M) ... northpeak (primary / default): 640,000 remaining of 10,000,000. The 9.36M run is ~14.6× the remaining allowance — short by ~8.72M tokens. It cannot come close to finishing on northpeak.

report_2.evidence: Full run = transcript_count × avg_tokens_per_scoring_call = 5,200 × 1,800 = 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M) ... northpeak (primary/default): only 640,000 of its 10M window remaining. The run needs 14.6× that — it overshoots by ~8.72M tokens. Primary could cover only ~355 of 5,200 transcripts before exhausting.

report_1.evidence: I'd route the run to the backend that can actually hold it — ridgeline, which has 10.8M remaining against a 9.36M need: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary

report_2.evidence: Instead I'd route the whole job to the secondary backend, which has capacity now: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary ... Ridgeline has 10.8M remaining vs. 9.36M needed

report_1.evidence: I would not launch via the default path. As configured, run_nightly.sh would fire all 9.36M tokens at northpeak, which has only 640K left ... Splitting isn't worth it here ... ridgeline comfortably holds the whole job — so an all-ridgeline run is the cleaner, lower-risk choice.

report_2.evidence: I would not run the default path. It targets northpeak, which is nearly exhausted (640K left, ~93.6% of its window already spent as of the 19:25Z snapshot), so a 9.36M-token full run would blow the budget and die partway (~355 transcripts in).

report_1.evidence: The snapshot is ~3h stale. Both windows are still in the same pre-morning cycle (no reset between 19:25 and 22:30) ... Per project documentation's "don't trust a stale signal" rule, I'd re-pull live usage before committing; ridgeline is still clearly the safer of the two either way.
